business.headline: As milk, fuel and gold get costlier, here are 7 everyday expenses families may cutThe grocery bill feels heavier. Filling the petrol tank costs more. Even an occasional gold purchase suddenly seems expensive. Across India, rising prices are pinching household budgets, leaving many families asking: What everyday expenses can we cut without making life harder? Let's have a look.

Before you file your ITR this year, don't miss these details in AIS and Form 26ASAre you about to file your income tax return without checking AIS and Form 26AS? Before you hit submit, it may be worth taking a closer look, as even small mismatches in income or tax details can create problems later.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra shares jump: Why IT stocks gained up to 6% todayMajor IT stocks rebounded sharply on Tuesday, led by Infosys, TCS and midcap peers. The move reflected improving risk appetite, bargain hunting and renewed hopes of stronger US tech spending.

Explained: Why Adani Group stocks rose up to 5% todayAdani Group shares climbed in morning trade after the US moved to drop charges against Gautam Adani and his associates. The move, along with the LPG settlement, eased regulatory uncertainty and lifted investor confidence.

How the West Asia war triggered a global fight for India's oil marketRussia, Gulf producers and the United States are competing harder for India's oil market as the West Asia war disrupts trade routes. The scramble underlines India's growing leverage as one of the few big economies where oil demand is still rising.

Petrol, diesel prices rise again: Will 2022's 90-paise hike formula return?State-run oil companies have raised petrol and diesel prices again across major cities. The move has revived talk of a 2022-style pattern of small, frequent fuel hikes.

Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 23,700; Infosys up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 365.07 points, or 0.48%, to 75,680.11 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 107.55 points, or 0.45%, to 23,757.50, as of 9:40 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock market to crash after 2nd hike in fuel prices?Markets are set for a mildly positive open even after a second fuel price hike in a week. Hopes of easing tensions around Iran are offering support, but high crude prices and volatility are keeping traders cautious.

Standard Chartered plans 7,000 job cuts amid AI push and higher profit targetsStandard Chartered said it will cut more than 7,000 roles by 2030 as it leans on automation and artificial intelligence. The bank is tying the move to higher return targets and a bigger push in wealth management.

8th Pay Commission: The food-cost formula behind salary revision demandsEmployee unions have asked the 8th Pay Commission to recalculate minimum pay using updated food-cost and nutrition standards. The push shows how salary revision talks are increasingly centred on inflation, household budgets and daily living costs.

Petrol, diesel prices increased by 90 paise, second hike in 5 daysThe latest revision of prices follows a Rs 3-per-litre hike announced just days earlier, adding to the burden on consumers already facing high transport and household costs.

US drops fraud charges against Adani amid proposed $10 billion investment planThe move is being seen as the latest example of the Trump administration stepping away from high-profile prosecutions initiated during former President Joe Biden's administration.

India needs to triple its strategic petroleum reserves: Energy expert Anas AlhajjiIn an exclusive interview with India Today, energy expert Anas Alhajji spoke on the global oil crisis caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz due to the West Asia war and its impact on the economy and more

Bengaluru engineer, 25, has net worth of Rs 40 lakh. Here's how he built itNo inheritance, no family wealth and no shortcuts, yet a young Bengaluru engineer says he managed to build Rs 40 lakh in net worth surprisingly early. His story, shared on Reddit, is now catching attention for its simple money lessons.

Be remembered for Good: Seagram's 100 Pipers Travel Gear and India Today group are spotlighting stories that matterExploring the stories of individuals who are using their influence, work, and intent to create real-world impact.

India will keep buying Russian oil despite US waiver changes, says govt officialIndia said it will continue buying crude oil from Russia regardless of the US sanctions waiver position. The statement underlined New Delhi's focus on energy security and commercially viable supplies amid elevated oil prices.

How India's richest are investing differently, and what you can learn from itAlternative Investment Funds are structured differently. Category I and Category III AIFs, in particular, allow fund managers to run concentrated portfolios with fewer stocks, higher conviction, and deeper research.
Sensex recovers to end 77 points up, Nifty above 23,600; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex added 77.05 points to end at 75,315.04, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 6.45 points to close at 23,649.95.

Markets under pressure, rupee weakens. Are index funds a safer long-term bet?Sharp swings in equities, a weaker rupee and higher crude prices have pushed investors to reassess their strategy. Analysts say Nifty 50 index funds are drawing attention as a lower-cost long-term option, though volatility remains high.

What salary do you need to buy a home in Delhi-NCR now?A Rs 1 crore budget today may not fetch a decent home in many parts of Gurgaon or Delhi, while buyers earning even Rs 20 lakh annually are struggling to balance EMIs, savings, and lifestyle costs.

entertainment.headline: Shruti Haasan to turn writer for father Kamal Haasan's Hollywood film? Unconfirmed reports say Kamal Haasan may headline a Hollywood film scripted by Shruti Haasan. The buzz has intensified as he returns from politics to a crowded film slate and recent US visits.

Main Vaapas Aaunga trailer: Diljit Dosanjh brings a love torn apart by Partition The trailer of Main Vaapas Aaunga is out. The film, set in the Partition-era, is about a tale of love, memory and an enduring sense of home.

Daisy Shah recalls encounters with drunk fans at events: They're reeking of alcohol Daisy Shah recalled uncomfortable fan encounters at public events, including situations where people under the influence of alcohol made her uneasy.

Shivaleeka Oberoi, Abhishek Pathak name their daughter Aarika. Here's what it means Actor Shivaleeka Oberoi and her husband, director Abhishek Pathak, have introduced their daughter Aarika in a joint post. The announcement marked one month since her birth. They also revealed the meaning of her name.

Inside Khatron Ke Khiladi 15: Gaurav Khanna shares fun behind-the-scenes moments Actor Gaurav Khanna has begun sharing light moments with fellow contestants from the Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 shoot in Cape Town. The posts offer a playful break from the show's usual high-stakes image and are fuelling early curiosity around the new season.

Karuppu makers apologise, remove dialogue mocking Ilaiyaraaja's copyright fight The makers of Suriya's Karuppu have apologised for a dialogue referencing Ilaiyaraaja and promised to remove it. The move follows online criticism, even as the team says the scene was not meant to offend.

After The Kashmir Files and The Bengal Files, now comes The Maharashtra Files The trailer for The Maharashtra Files frames a highway project around a bitter land acquisition battle. It places corruption, unequal compensation and demands for accountability at the centre of the film.

Anupam Kher backs PM's fuel-saving appeal, takes Vande Bharat from Jaipur to Delhi Anupam Kher backed Prime Minister Narendra Modi's fuel-saving appeal and took the Vande Bharat from Jaipur to Delhi. He said small changes such as public transport and carpooling can make a meaningful difference.

Actor Basil Joseph predicts Sanju Samson's six, gets it right again. Watch Malayalam actor Basil Joseph predicted Sanju Samson's six from the stands during the CSK vs SRH match at Chepauk. The clip went viral again, echoing his earlier spot-on call from the T20 World Cup final.

Govinda steps in as security guard gets into heated exchange with paps. Video Govinda stepped in to calm an argument between his security guard and a paparazzo at an event in Mumbai. Videos of the incident soon went viral on social media.

Twisha Sharma's co-star, director react as SIT probes actor's death in Bhopal Actor-model Twisha Sharma's death has shocked colleagues from the film industry, even as police probe allegations of dowry harassment and abetment of suicide against her husband and mother-in-law.

Brilliant Minds: Why Zachary Quinto's medical drama felt more human than most shows NBC has cancelled Brilliant Minds after two seasons, ending Zachary Quinto's reflective medical drama. Its quiet, humane take on neurology leaves behind an unfinished but affecting legacy.

Alia, Sharvari on India's Got Latent 2? Viral pic from Samay Raina show sparks buzz A picture of Alia Bhatt and Sharvari from the sets of Samay Raina's India's Got Latent Season 2 has surfaced online. The viral image comes months after the show became embroiled in controversy, which eventually led to all its episodes being removed from YouTube.

Karuppu box office Day 4: Suriya's phenomenal run continues with first Monday Suriya's long-awaited box office comeback may finally be here. After a sensational first Monday hold, Karuppu has stormed past Singam 2 to become the biggest hit of the actor's career worldwide.

Pati Patni... box office Day 4: Film sees sharp Monday dip after steady weekend Pati Patni Aur Woh Do posed Rs 3.25 crore nett on its first Monday after a steady opening weekend. The 58.1 per cent fall took its four-day India nett total to Rs 20.75 crore and worldwide gross to Rs 28.84 crore.

Gracie Cochrane exits Harry Potter series, HBO to recast Ginny Weasley for S2 Gracie Cochrane has exited HBO's upcoming Harry Potter reboot "due to unforeseen circumstances," prompting the makers to recast Ginny Weasley for season two. The development has sparked curiosity among fans as the character becomes central to the events of Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets.

Karuppu gets CM Vijay's wishes after Suriya-Trisha film's strong box office start Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Joseph Vijay conveyed his wishes to producer SR Prabu for Karuppu's success. The film, starring Suriya and Trisha, grossed Rs 147 crore worldwide at the box office.

Karuppu: Suriya is back in vintage form and the box office is loving it After dabbling in genres for over a decade, Suriya's latest, Karuppu, is a fan-service that brings him back to familiar turf. With nostalgic nods to past hits, the film is Suriya's first bonafide hit since Singam II in 2013.

Tom Kane, voice of The Powerpuff Girls and Star Wars characters, dies at 64 Tom Kane, best known as the voice of Professor Utonium in The Powerpuff Girls, died at the age of 64 due to complications from a stroke he suffered in 2020.

general news.headline: Twisha Sharma's parents pushed her into glamour, lived off her looks: Mother-in-lawTwisha Sharma's mother-in-law accused her parents of pushing her into the glamour world and profiting from her image. The remarks come as Twisha's death in Bhopal remains under scrutiny amid her family's dowry harassment allegations.

Government pushes back on Norwegian reporter's rights question in tense exchangeDuring a media interaction in Oslo, MEA Secretary (West) Sibi George defended India's democratic record and media landscape. He said foreign observers often misunderstand the country by relying on selective reports.

Prabhakaran, Tamil identity and TVK: Is Vijay building a neo-Dravidian project?Repeatedly invoking the memory of Prabhakaran and the emotional legacy of the Tamil cause, Vijay appears to be crafting a new ideological synthesis of the welfare-driven legacy of Dravidianism with the cultural assertiveness of Tamil nationalism.

Bulldozers in action in Kolkata; more to follow across Bengal, says BJP govtThe BJP government of West Bengal led by CM Suvendu Adhikari has launched anti-encroachment drives in and around Kolkata. Bulldozers have rolled into localities of Kolkata, including Sealdah and Howrah railway stations. Bengal minister Dilip Ghosh said that the drives in Kolkata were just the beginning, and more bulldozer action across the state would follow.

Why Amit Shah chose a conflict zone to announce Naxal-free IndiaBastar, one of India's most backward regions, had become synonymous with Naxalism. By choosing to declare India 'Naxal-free' from Bastar, Amit Shah attempted to signal that the region, once seen as a symbol of fear, was now a place of stability.

Do Indians listen to PM Modi? Lakshadweep tourism numbers tell a storyRTI data accessed by India Today shows that tourist arrivals in Lakshadweep surged from just 3,875 in 2020 to a record 68,328 in 2024. The sharpest jump came after PM Modi's January 2024 visit to the islands.

A storm is coming: Rahul Gandhi says brace for economic shock in swipe at PMRahul Gandhi attacked Prime Minister Narendra Modi over his ongoing five-nation tour, warning of an impending economic shock that he said would hit the common man in Uttar Pradesh and across the country.

Papa, save me: Gwalior woman dies 20 minutes after call, family alleges dowry deathPalak Rajak died at her matrimonial home in Gwalior shortly after a distress call to her father. Her family alleged dowry harassment and murder, while police said forensic and postmortem findings would guide action.

Pak restores pre-Partition names of Lahore streets: Islampura is now Krishan NagarAcross Lahore, names that survived only in memory are slowly finding their way back onto the city's walls again after 79 years.

Twisha Sharma death case: Chilling chats, absconding husband, powerful in-lawsTwisha Sharma was found hanging at her matrimonial home in Bhopal, prompting a dowry death case against her husband and mother-in-law. Her family's allegations, chat records and the bail proceedings have made the investigation legally and politically sensitive.

Euthanise if necessary: Supreme Court's big order to curb stray dog attacksIn its November 2025 order, the Supreme Court of India directed authorities to remove stray dogs from public institutions and transport hubs, and relocate them to shelters instead of releasing them back.

Pak officer claims missiles struck 2 Indian airbases. They don't even existIn an undated interview with a local Pakistani channel, an officer associated with the Fatah-1 operations claimed that the strikes successfully hit two Indian military targets — "Rajouri Airbase" and "Mamun Airbase." However, the two airbases do not exist.

Dear NTA, burnout, PCOD and 2 attempts later I am back in Kota because of youA NEET aspirant from Jamshedpur recounts years of sacrifice after her family moved to Kota for preparation. After battling health issues, isolation, and academic pressure, she is now studying again without her handwritten notes, reflecting the uncertainty and emotional toll faced by NEET aspirants.

CBSE re-evaluation portal draws complaints as Class 12 students report login issuesSeveral CBSE Class 12 students complained about login issues and alleged glitches on the re-evaluation portal after the application window opened on May 19. However, the board has maintained that the system is functioning properly and only minor technical issues were reported.

UP civic body chief seen offering Rs 1 crore bounty for TMC MP, calls clip fakeA viral video allegedly shows BJP municipal chairman Pradeep Dixit announcing a Rs 1 crore reward for the beheading of Trinamool Congress MP Saayoni Ghosh during a protest in Sikandrabad. Dixit has denied making the statement, claiming the footage was AI-manipulated to malign him amid a revived row over Saayoni 's old social media post.

Main Vaapas Aaunga trailer: Diljit Dosanjh brings a love torn apart by PartitionThe trailer of Main Vaapas Aaunga is out. The film, set in the Partition-era, is about a tale of love, memory and an enduring sense of home.

Janhvi Kapoor gives the saree-dupatta combo a twist and we are obsessedJanhvi Kapoor has a way with sarees. This time, for the Peddi trailer launch, she opted for an icy blue custom Manish Malhotra saree with a duppata twist, and we are loving it!

IPS officer sidelined by Mamata over Park Street rape given key role in BJP govtDamayanti Sen, a 1996-batch IPS officer, came into prominence during the 2012 Park Street rape case, which snowballed into a major political controversy after a woman alleged she was gangraped inside a moving car after leaving a nightclub in Kolkata's Park Street area.

Samson's Dhoni-esque stumping rattles Heinrich Klaasen. SRH batter abuses keeperSanju Samson's lightning-fast stumping saw the back of Heinrich Klaasen in CSK vs SRH. Tempers flared as Samson stared down the batter, who slipped in some words while leaving the ground.

Is Delhi moving to Metro travel to save fuel? What visits to 8 stations revealedA reality check across eight Delhi Metro stations found commuters responding unevenly to the Metro Monday campaign. While some said they had cut private vehicle use to save fuel, others pointed to crowding, weak last-mile links and doubts over political optics.

130 passengers applaud US pilot after he truthfully explains why flight was delayedA US pilot used the gate microphone to explain why an Atlanta-bound flight was being delayed. His frank safety warning and defence of gate staff drew applause from passengers and praise online.

How the West Asia war triggered a global fight for India's oil marketRussia, Gulf producers and the United States are competing harder for India's oil market as the West Asia war disrupts trade routes. The scramble underlines India's growing leverage as one of the few big economies where oil demand is still rising.

CCTV shows Twisha Sharma heading to roof, husband carrying her body an hour laterExclusive CCTV footage, claims of psychiatric treatment, allegations of dowry harassment and a missing husband have turned Twisha Sharma's death into one of Madhya Pradesh's most explosive and emotionally charged investigations in recent weeks.

How SC dismantled railways' decade-long defence that it needn't pay electricity surchargesThe financial toll of the verdict will be astounding as the railways risks losing the entire savings achieved through electrification

sports.headline: India squad in good shape for Women's T20 World Cup: Smriti Mandhana Vice-captain Smriti Mandhana on Tuesday avoided revealing too much about the Indian women's team's preparations for next month's T20 World Cup, but said the squad is in good condition ahead of the 12-team event.

Ruturaj will be a fine captain: Fleming backs CSK skipper amid transition period Chennai Super Kings head coach Stephen Fleming admitted Ruturaj Gaikwad is still learning as a captain after the team's IPL playoff hopes took a major hit with the defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad.

Harbouring Olympic dreams, Puja Tomar eyes impact beyond UFC octagon for India India's UFC trailblazer Puja Tomar opens up on her growth as a fighter, India's untapped MMA potential, her dream of launching an academy, and why representing the country at the Olympics would be the ultimate honour.

RR vs LSG: Can rattled Royals settle down and push for playoffs? RR vs LSG Preview: Rajasthan Royals head back home in Jaipur to play their penultimate league game of the season. Can they keep their heads and make a final push for the playoffs this season?

IND v AFG squad selection live updates: Will selectors drop Rohit for ODIs? India (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) ODI, one-off Test squad announcement live updates: Inida will play a three-match ODI series and a one-off Test against Afghanistan, days affter IPL 2026. Selectors are sweating over the fitness of Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya as the BCCI is all set to announce the squads on Tuesday, May 19.

MS Dhoni might not have played in 2026, but he's still important for CSK: Fleming Chennai Super Kings head coach Stephen Fleming defended MS Dhoni's time away from the game in IPL 2026, while Suresh Raina revealed he is actively pressing the franchise legend for a 2027 comeback.

IND vs AFG: Rohit Sharma, Hardik Pandya's availability hinges on fitness clearance Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya are awaiting medical clearance before the Afghanistan ODI series. Their fitness status has left India's team management seeking clarity on two senior players.

Big Bash League set for historic debut in India, BCCI gives final green light Chennai will host a Big Bash League match. The fixture, after months of talks, signals Cricket Australia's push into India with the BCCI's approval.

Hardik Pandya trains at full intensity in Kolkata. Is a return on cards? KKR vs MI: Hardik Pandya trained at full intensity ahead of MI's IPL match against KKR on Wednesday. Returning from a back spasm, the skipper bowled at full tilt amid rumours, even as MI stands knocked out of the playoff race.

Pep Guardiola to leave Manchester City, ex-Chelsea boss expected to take over Pep Guardiola is reportedly set to leave Manchester City, ending a trophy-laden era at the Etihad, with former assistant Enzo Maresca emerging as the leading candidate to replace him.

Play of the Day: Gaikwad, CSK get outfoxed by oldest trick in the playbookCSK vs SRH, IPL Play of the Day: The oldest trick in the book helped SRH outfox CSK, leaving Chennai's play-off hopes hanging by a thread.

Neymar's World Cup 2026 dream becomes reality, included in Brazil's 26-man squadCarlo Ancelotti has brought Neymar back into Brazil's 26-man World Cup 2026 squad after a long absence. The call ends months of uncertainty over his international future as Brazil chase a sixth title.

5 teams, 1 spot: IPL 2026 playoffs scenarios for 4th place explainedIPL 2026, CSK vs SRH: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat the Chennai Super Kings on Monday at the Chepauk to qualify for the playoffs. The win also took the Gujarat Titans through, meaning that only one playoff spot is up for grabs.

After disaster innings, CSK captain gives bizarre explanation for loss to SRHRuturaj Gaikwad blamed Chennai Super Kings' bowlers after the defeat to SunRisers Hyderabad. His remarks drew criticism as his own slow innings came under scrutiny in a must-win match.

MS Dhoni may play IPL 2027 after missed call in 2026, hints Suresh RainaIPL 2026, CSK vs SRH: Suresh Raina hinted that MS Dhoni could still have another IPL season left in him, despite the Chennai Super Kings legend missing all 13 matches for the franchise so far this season.

Whistle, poda: Ishan Kishan send-off to CSK fans goes viralJeered throughout his innings against Chennai Super Kings, Ishan Kishan decided to give the CSK fans a send-off after guiding SRH to a win on Monday. Kishan's gesture towards the fans went viral after the game.

Sun rises in Chepauk as Hyderabad grit it out to seal a playoff berthIPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad sealed their place in the IPL 2026 playoffs with a composed five-wicket win over Chennai Super Kings at Chepauk. While the night carried emotional weight around the possible final home chapter of MS Dhoni, Ishan Kishan and Pat Cummins ensured the spotlight ultimately belonged to their playoff-clinching performance.

Samson's Dhoni-esque stumping rattles Heinrich Klaasen. SRH batter abuses keeperSanju Samson's lightning-fast stumping saw the back of Heinrich Klaasen in CSK vs SRH. Tempers flared as Samson stared down the batter, who slipped in some words while leaving the ground.

Who is Karman Kaur Thandi? India's former No.1 set for French Open debutKarman Kaur Thandi enters the 2026 French Open qualifiers using a protected ranking, with the former India No.1 set to face France's Harmony Tan in her opening-round clash. The 27-year-old, once ranked No. 196, is aiming for her first-ever Grand Slam main-draw appearance.

Archery: 4-time Olympian Deepika Kumari fails to qualify for Asian GamesIndia has finalised its archery team for the Asian Games. After a gruelling 3-day trials in Sonipat, Haryana, 4-time Olympian Deepika Kumari has failed to qualify for the squad.

15 runs in 21 balls, 0 boundaries: CSK fans snap at Ruturaj GaikwadChennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad played an absolute howler in their last home game of the season. Gaikwad lasted 21 balls where he had a strike-rate of 71.

Cirstea breaks new ground in farewell season, becomes oldest top 20 debutantSorana Cirstea's farewell season saw a historic surge as she reached the Italian Open semi-finals and became the oldest debutant in the WTA Top 20 at 36. Her run included a win over world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka before ending with a 6–4, 6–3 loss to Coco Gauff.

Urvil Patel pays tribute to MS Dhoni at Chepauk, helicopters Cummins out of groundIPL 2026: Urvil Patel uncorked a Dhoni-style helicopter shot off Pat Cummins in CSK's last home game of the season. MS Dhoni was again deemed unfit for the game against Sunrisers Hyderabad on Monday, May 17.

After Mirpur brainfade, Pakistan double down, confident of chasing 437 in SylhetBAN vs PAK, 2nd Test: Pakistan are confident of chasing 437 in the Sylhet Test, with bowling coach Umar Gul insisting the batting unit has the firepower and conditions are favourable on the second and third days.

MS Dhoni misses CSK's last home game, but retirement rumours dashed againIPL 2026: MS Dhoni travelled with Chennai Super Kings but stayed unavailable for the must-win home game against Sunrisers Hyderabad. His fitness issues have kept Chepauk waiting again and left only a faint chance of a return later.

Complete disaster: Ex-India head coach's slams Indian football administrationManolo Marquez has again criticised the way Indian football is being run as FC Goa prepare to face Kerala Blasters. With the ISL calendar unresolved and contracts nearing expiry, he says he cannot see a clear future for himself in India.

CSK vs SRH Highlights: Hyderabad qualify for play-offs, carry Gujarat along with themChennai (CSK) vs Hyderabad (SRH) Live Score, IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad have qualified for the playoffs after beating Chennai Super Kings by five wickets on Monday. With SRH's win, the Gujarat Titans also secured their berth in the top four.

Mushfiqur Rahim century leaves Pakistan with a mountain to climb in Sylhet TestBAN vs PAK, 2nd Test Day 3: Bangladesh dominated Day 3 of the Sylhet Test, setting Pakistan a massive target of 437 runs after Mushfiqur Rahim's brilliant 137 and Litton Das's fluent 69 in the second innings.

Arshdeep is fully fit: Punjab Kings contradict themselves on pacer's fitnessIPL 2026: Punjab Kings have found themselves under scrutiny once again after claiming that Arshdeep Singh is fully fit, contrary to media 'reports'. This comes just 48 hours after their own physio said the fast bowler had been playing with a niggle midway through the season.

India squad for Afghanistan series: Bumrah's workload, Rohit's fitness in focusIndia's squad selection for next month's one-off Test and ODI series against Afghanistan is set to revolve around the fitness of Rohit Sharma and the workload management of Jasprit Bumrah, with selectors also expected to discuss fresh faces like Prince Yadav and Aquib Nabi ahead of a crucial home season.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
